description_long: This is a short wooden fence section constructed from roughly hewn planks, featuring two thick vertical posts and two horizontal rails. The wood appears unfinished, with a natural light brown color and visible grain texture. The central section of the top rail is broken, with a jagged plank hanging downward, suggesting damage or disrepair. The fence is simple and utilitarian, lacking any decorative elements or embellishments. The posts have flat tops, and the rails are unevenly aligned, contributing to a rustic, weathered appearance. This type of fence is commonly found in parks, gardens, or as a temporary barrier in urban or suburban settings.
description: A rustic, damaged wooden fence section with two posts and two horizontal rails, featuring a broken top rail. The wood is light brown and unfinished, giving it a weathered, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broken top rail and the two vertical posts with flat tops.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the alignment of the rails.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the broken rail and posts visible from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the staggered rails.
